title: Lab 9: BlueBikes Spatial Story
description: An interactive Mapbox + D3 exploration of BlueBikes station demand, directional flow, and bike-access isochrones.
story: This lab combined map rendering, SVG overlays, and temporal filtering. The key challenge was keeping station positions and isochrone paths synchronized with map pan and zoom while preserving readable visual encoding.

title: Freshman Fall Study Planner
description: A simple weekly planner app that helped me balance psets, lectures, and club meetings.
story: This was the first project where I stopped writing code only for class and built something I actually used every week. I learned quickly that a feature is only useful if entering data takes less effort than remembering everything in my head.

title: Dorm Chore Rotation Tool
description: A lightweight scheduling tool for assigning cleaning tasks fairly across roommates.
story: Our floor chat was full of friendly arguments about whose turn it was to clean. Building this tool taught me that transparent rules and visible history can solve social friction faster than extra reminders.

title: Campus Event Discovery Map
description: An interactive map that aggregates club and department events by location and time.
story: I wanted a single place to find events between classes instead of checking five different mailing lists. The project pushed me to think about spatial filtering and how small UX decisions affect whether people browse or bounce.

title: Recipe Cost Optimizer
description: A grocery planning app that compares recipes by cost, prep time, and pantry overlap.
story: I started this after realizing I was buying the same ingredients twice in one week. Building it taught me to model real-world constraints and to prioritize a clean data entry flow over flashy UI elements.

title: Open Source CLI Contribution
description: Contributed bug fixes and docs improvements to a command-line tooling project.
story: Working in a public repo changed how I write code comments and commit messages. The review process was strict but fair, and it improved the way I justify technical decisions.

title: Housing Listings ETL Pipeline
description: A weekly pipeline that extracts listing data, cleans address fields, and tracks price changes.
story: This was my first project where data quality bugs mattered more than model complexity. The biggest wins came from robust parsing, deduplication, and checks that caught broken records early.

title: Portfolio Site Redesign
description: A redesign focused on reusable components, cleaner navigation, and better mobile behavior.
story: I treated this as a maintainability project rather than a cosmetic refresh. Consolidating repeated markup into reusable components made future updates faster and less error-prone.

title: Accessibility Audit Toolkit
description: A checklist and script bundle for checking color contrast, heading structure, and keyboard navigation.
story: A teammate pointed out that one of my pages was hard to use with keyboard-only navigation. This toolkit came from that feedback and made accessibility checks part of my normal workflow.

title: Narrative Dashboard Prototype
description: A prototype that pairs short narrative text with synchronized visual cards for story-driven analysis.
story: I wanted to test whether sequencing text and visuals could guide readers without feeling rigid. Iterating on pacing and annotation density helped me balance explanation with exploration.

title: Personal Analytics Dashboard
description: A dashboard for tracking reading habits, project time, and weekly progress trends.
story: This project closed the loop between data collection and reflection. Seeing trends over time changed how I planned my week and reinforced the value of simple metrics that are easy to maintain.
